revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Isagawa Awa jinja]][[ja:率川阿波神社]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|率川阿波神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Isagawa Awa Shrine | native_name = 率川阿波神社 | image = Isagawa Awa jinja 1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isagawa Awa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.681418|135.825898}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Isagawa Awa Shrine'''|率川阿波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]] and part of [[Isagawa Shrine]]
== Isagawa Awa Shrine == Isagawa Awa Shrine is a subshrine dedicated to [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]]. It is located between Sumiyoshi Shrine and Kasuga Shrine, which are branch shrines of Isagawa Shrine.
It is said to have been erected by {{ill|Dainagon|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainagon|qq=}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Korekimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Korekimi}} in the second year of the {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} era ( {{ill|771|en|771|qid=Q29950|lt=771|1=771}} ), and in the second year of {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|qid=Q1373077|lt=the Jinshu|1=Ninju}} era ( [[S.852|852]] ), he was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank}} . A small shrine (shikinai shosha) listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] as " '''Isagawa Awa Shrine''' " . The enshrined deity is Kotoshironushi no Mikoto, commonly known as [[Ebisu]], and in addition to the regular festival on June 17th, the first Ebisu festival is held on January 5th . [[File:Old_Isagawa-Awa_Shrine_(NishiJodo-cho,_Nara)_2.jpg|thumb|The former site of Otogawa Awa Shrine in Nishikido-machi]] It was destroyed by a peasant uprising in {{ill|1532|en|1532|qid=Q6436|lt=1532|1=1532}} and by fire during the wars waged by the {{ill|Miyoshi Trio|ja|三好三人衆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Trio}} and {{ill|Matsunaga Hisahide|en|Matsunaga Hisahide|qid=Q1143038|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} . According to a mid- {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} book by {{ill|Murai Kodō|ja|村井古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murai Kodō}} " {{nihongo|Narabō's clumsy explanation|奈良坊目拙解|Narabō Mokusetsukai}} ," the old site was located on the south side of Nishijodo-cho, behind the second house from the east, and a single {{ill|Pine|en|Pine|qid=Q12024|lt=pine tree|1=Pine}} was enshrined as a sacred tree, but it had fallen into severe disrepair. It was not until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} that a small shrine was enshrined, and in {{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} ( {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taisho|1=Taishō era}} 9), the main shrine building was moved to the grounds of Isagawa Shrine.
{{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=In 1959|1=1959}} ( {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 34), due to development of the temple grounds, it was moved to its current location along with the subordinate shrines Sumiyoshi Shrine and Kasuga Shrine.
Currently, in front of the shrine building, there is a square stone lantern with the inscription " Awa Shrine, 1803 (3rd year of the Kyoho era )" that was moved from its previous location.
=== Awa Shrine === <gallery mode="packed"> File:Isagawa_Awa_Jinja_Nara.jpg|Awa Jinja File:Isagawa_Awa_Jinja_Torii_Nara.jpg|Awa Jinja Torii File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_Torii_2023.jpg|View 10 File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_1.jpg|View 11 File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_2.jpg|View 12 File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_3.jpg|View 13 File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_4.jpg|View 14 File:Isagawa_Awa_jinja_5.jpg|View 15 File:Isagawa-jinja,_keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha </gallery>
